headline: Why Fortinet (FTNT) Is Up 6.7% After Q1 Beat And Raised AI Security Outlook
summary: Fortinet reported its first-quarter 2026 results on 6 May, with revenue rising to US$1,849.6 million and net income to US$534.5 million, alongside higher earnings per share versus a year earlier. Beyond the headline beat and raised guidance, Fortinet underscored the growing role of AI-enabled security and new FortiGate G-series appliances in driving demand across data centers and operational technology environments. headline: Is the Fortinet Stock Rally Sustainable?
summary: Fortinet stock (FTNT) surged to $104 in extended trading following its first quarter 2026 earnings report on May 6, 2026. This "beat and raise" performance saw revenue hit $1.85 billion, representing 20.1% year-over-year growth and surpassing analyst estimates of $1.77 billion. While the market frequently rewards top-line growth, the underlying shift toward AI-driven security suggests a fundamental change in Fortinet's trajectory.

content.title: Is the Fortinet Stock Rally Sustainable?
content.summary: Fortinet stock (FTNT) surged to $104 in extended trading following its first quarter 2026 earnings report on May 6, 2026. This "beat and raise" performance saw revenue hit $1.85 billion, representing 20.1% year-over-year growth and surpassing analyst estimates of $1.77 billion. While the market frequently rewards top-line growth, the underlying shift toward AI-driven security suggests a fundamental change in Fortinet's trajectory.
